findings.title: Verification scope is too narrow after constructor refactor
findings.detail: Task 0 Step 5 runs only 6 test files: test_compose_service_structure.py, test_compose_loop_test_driver.py, test_compose_loop_persistence.py, test_compose_loop_tool_call_cap.py, test_audit_failure_primacy.py, and test_compose_loop_invariants.py. Moving the Phase 3 monkeypatched __init__ into the real constructor sets 8 instance attributes (_max_tool_calls_per_turn, _telemetry, _redaction_telemetry, _phase3_last_tool_outcomes, _phase3_last_expected_current_state_id, _phase3_last_redacted_assistant_tool_calls, _phase3_last_redacted_tool_rows, _phase3_last_audit_outcome). Any AttributeError from a missed attribute initialization would only surface in tests that actually reach those code paths. At least 15 test files read _phase3_last_* or _max_tool_calls_per_turn attributes, and at least 8 files (test_compose_loop_anti_anchor.py, test_compose_loop_audit_wiring.py, test_compose_loop_envelope.py, test_compose_loop_llm_audit.py, test_compose_loop_unknown_tool_name.py, test_advisor_tool.py, test_service.py, test_provider_cache_markers.py) instantiate ComposerServiceImpl() without calling _run_one_turn_for_test and are not in the Step 5 list. Behavioral regression could be invisible until CI runs the full suite. Task 9 runs only npm frontend tests, so the gap is not closed at the end of the phase.
findings.evidence: grep -rl '_phase3_last|_max_tool_calls_per_turn|_telemetry|_redaction_telemetry' tests/unit/web/composer/*.py tests/property/web/composer/*.py → 20 files; grep -rln 'ComposerServiceImpl(' tests/unit/web/composer/*.py tests/property/web/composer/*.py → 10 files. Only 6 appear in Step 5.
findings.recommendation: Replace the Step 5 allow-list with: PYTHONPATH=src uv run pytest -q tests/unit/web/composer/ tests/property/web/composer/ This runs all composer tests as the cleanup-correctness gate. If full-suite time is a concern, the allow-list must at minimum expand to include all 10 files that instantiate ComposerServiceImpl() and all 15 files that read phase3 instance attributes.

findings.title: AST guard does not cover the _PHASE3_ORIGINAL_COMPOSER_INIT module-level capture
findings.detail: service.py line 4225 assigns: _PHASE3_ORIGINAL_COMPOSER_INIT = ComposerServiceImpl.__init__  This is a module-level Name assignment (not a ComposerServiceImpl.X = Y Attribute assignment), so the forbidden_assignments set in the AST guard will not catch it. If a developer removes line 4247 (the patch application) but leaves line 4225, the guard passes with dead code in place. Additionally, the Step 6 rg-check only searches for 'ComposerServiceImpl._* =' patterns and will also miss this case.
findings.evidence: src/elspeth/web/composer/service.py line 4225: _PHASE3_ORIGINAL_COMPOSER_INIT = ComposerServiceImpl.__init__. The guard in Task 0 Step 1 only checks ast.Attribute nodes where target.value is an ast.Name — it does not check for module-level ast.Name targets matching the _PHASE3_* pattern.
findings.recommendation: Extend the AST guard with a second loop: for node in ast.walk(tree): if isinstance(node, ast.Assign): for target in node.targets: if isinstance(target, ast.Name) and target.id.startswith('_PHASE3_'): hits.append(target.id). Also update the Step 6 rg-check to include the _PHASE3_ORIGINAL pattern: ! rg -n '_PHASE3_ORIGINAL_COMPOSER_INIT' src/elspeth/web/composer/service.py

findings.title: Nested failed_turn fields not asserted in recovery payload parsing tests
findings.detail: _failed_turn_response_body() in routes.py line 1293-1298 returns exactly four fields: assistant_message_id, tool_calls_attempted, tool_responses_persisted, transcript_url. The Task 1 Step 1 test specification covers the top-level ApiError recovery fields (partial_state, failed_turn, partial_state_save_failed, partial_state_save_error) but does not require asserting that the nested failed_turn object contains all four sub-fields. A client.ts parse bug that drops tool_calls_attempted or truncates the failed_turn object to just assistant_message_id and transcript_url would not be caught. This also means the TypeScript FailedTurn type in Task 1 Step 2 could be defined with missing fields and no test would fail.
findings.evidence: routes.py lines 1293-1298 return assistant_message_id, tool_calls_attempted, tool_responses_persisted, transcript_url. Plan Task 1 Step 1 only lists partial_state, failed_turn, partial_state_save_failed, partial_state_save_error at the top level.
findings.recommendation: Add assertions in the Task 1 Step 1 test that the parsed ApiError.failed_turn object contains all four sub-fields: assert(parsed.failed_turn?.assistant_message_id !== undefined), assert(parsed.failed_turn?.tool_calls_attempted !== undefined), assert(parsed.failed_turn?.tool_responses_persisted !== undefined), assert(parsed.failed_turn?.transcript_url === null for the null case).

findings.title: Test does not assert that fetchRecoveryTranscript constructs include_tool_rows=true in the URL
findings.detail: The backend audit recording (record_audit_grade_view_async) is gated on include_tool_rows being present and true in the HTTP query string (routes.py line 4173). The Task 5 Step 1 test spec says 'calls fetchRecoveryTranscript(sessionId, { limit: 500 })' — this tests the function name, not the wire. There is no required assertion that the constructed URL contains include_tool_rows=true. A future refactor of fetchRecoveryTranscript that silently omits the parameter would pass the test while breaking audit emission. The existing test_record_audit_grade_view.py and test_messages_route_include_tool_rows.py cover the backend side; the frontend test must close the client-side coupling.
findings.evidence: routes.py lines 4173-4180: if include_tool_rows: await service.record_audit_grade_view_async(...). tests/unit/web/sessions/test_record_audit_grade_view.py line 178: test_endpoint_emits_audit_log_when_include_tool_rows_true verifies the backend path but no frontend test pins the parameter.
findings.recommendation: Add an assertion in the RecoveryTranscript test (Task 5 Step 1) that the fetch mock was called with a URL matching /messages?.*include_tool_rows=true/ (or equivalent URLSearchParams inspection). This anchors the contract between the frontend helper and the backend audit gate.

findings.title: No test asserts redaction markers are visible in the rendered transcript (not raw sensitive content)
findings.detail: Task 5 Step 2 says 'never render hidden chain-of-thought or provider payloads' and Task 6 says 'View raw transcript' shows 'redacted-only' JSON. There is no required test in the plan that (a) confirms a transcript row containing a known <redacted-*> marker renders that marker in the DOM, and (b) confirms a transcript row containing a hypothetical raw sensitive value does NOT render it. Without both a positive and a negative assertion, a component that passes raw content through the display layer would not be caught by the test suite.
findings.evidence: Task 5 Step 1 covers loading states, null assistant_message_id, null transcript_url, and tool-call matching — but no redaction-content rendering assertion. Spec §7.6 says 'Expands to a read-only view of every redacted tool call/response JSON' without a test hook.
findings.recommendation: Add two test cases to RecoveryTranscript.test.tsx and/or RecoveryPanel.test.tsx: (1) input containing a row with content '<redacted-argument: summarized>' → asserts that string appears in the rendered output; (2) input containing a row whose raw_content field has a non-redacted value (simulating a row that should have been redacted) → asserts that raw_content is NOT rendered when include_raw_content is false (the default).

findings.title: discardRecovery test does not explicitly require clearing recoveryError from store state
findings.detail: Task 3 Step 1 requires testing that 'discardRecovery() closes the panel without API calls and without mutating compositionState'. It does not require asserting that the recoveryError field is set to null (or equivalent cleared state) after Discard. If discardRecovery only sets isOpen=false but leaves recoveryError populated, the next render cycle or the next sendMessage error might attempt to re-open a stale panel state. The store slice in Task 3 Step 2 includes recoveryError: ComposerRecoveryError | null — clearing it on Discard needs to be an explicit test obligation.
findings.evidence: Task 3 Step 2 defines recoveryError: ComposerRecoveryError | null and discardRecovery(). Task 3 Step 1 does not list 'recoveryError is null after discard' as a required assertion.
findings.recommendation: Add to the Task 3 Step 1 test list: 'after discardRecovery(), store.recoveryError === null'. Also verify recoveryStartedEditorRevision is cleared.

findings.title: Focus restoration tested for one close path; concurrent-edit cancel path not covered
findings.detail: Task 6 Step 1 requires 'uses useFocusTrap and restores focus on close'. The plan describes three close paths: (1) Discard, (2) Apply with no concurrent-edit conflict, (3) Apply → concurrent-edit confirmation → confirm-apply, and (4) Apply → concurrent-edit confirmation → cancel (panel remains open, confirmation dismissed). Focus restoration is a spec §7.7 requirement. The Task 6 Step 1 list does not explicitly require testing focus restoration for path (3) (after Apply completes) or path (4) (after cancelling the confirmation, focus should return to the concurrent-edit confirm button or the Apply button inside the panel, not to the background trigger).
findings.evidence: Task 6 Step 1: 'uses useFocusTrap and restores focus on close'. Task 6 Step 2 mentions ConfirmDialog/nested confirmation for concurrent edits. Spec §7.7 says 'Focus-trapped modal (existing Modal pattern)'.
findings.recommendation: Expand the Task 6 Step 1 test list to: 'restores focus to trigger element on Discard close', 'restores focus to trigger element on Apply (no confirmation)', 'restores focus to trigger element after Apply→confirm', 'focus remains inside panel and returns to Apply/confirmation buttons after Apply→cancel-confirmation'.

findings.title: Final phase gate omits backend Phase 3 test re-run
findings.detail: Task 9 Step 1 runs only the frontend suite (npm test). Task 0 Step 5 runs a narrow backend list; if a developer iterates on Task 0 while working through Tasks 1-7 and inadvertently reverts or drifts the Task 0 refactor, there is no backend gate in Task 9 to catch it. The final PR gate should prove the Phase 3 cleanup still holds at the end of the phase, not only at the end of Task 0.
findings.evidence: Task 9 Step 1: 'cd src/elspeth/web/frontend && npm test'. Task 9 has no Python test step. Task 0 Step 5 verifies cleanup but is not re-run in Task 9.
findings.recommendation: Add a Task 9 Step 0 (before npm test): PYTHONPATH=src uv run pytest -q tests/unit/web/composer/ tests/property/web/composer/ to ensure the Phase 4A backend cleanup is still valid at PR time. This addresses the W2 concern from prior reviews that recovery validation is VER-only; at minimum it keeps the backend clean.

findings.title: Accessibility audit is manual only — no automated a11y test in the suite
findings.detail: Task 8 Step 3 requires running axe or Lighthouse manually. The plan includes unit-level aria attribute assertions in Task 6 Step 1 (role=dialog, aria-modal=true, reason badge colour+text), but there is no automated a11y runner in the test suite (e.g., jest-axe or @testing-library/jest-dom a11y matchers). Manual-only audit means the a11y gate is not enforceable in CI and will silently regress if RecoveryPanel is later modified.
findings.evidence: Task 8 Step 3: 'Use axe or Lighthouse against the route where the panel is visible.' No jest-axe or equivalent is called in RecoveryPanel.test.tsx tests described in Task 6 Step 1.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding an automated axe scan in RecoveryPanel.test.tsx using jest-axe or vitest-axe: const { violations } = await axe(container); expect(violations).toHaveLength(0). Even one automated a11y gate prevents silent regression between manual audit cycles. This is not a blocker but substantially reduces the cost of future maintenance.

findings.title: No recovery-action audit event specified for Apply or Discard
findings.detail: CLAUDE.md states 'if it's not recorded, it didn't happen.' The transcript fetch triggers record_audit_grade_view_async server-side (confirmed in routes.py lines 4173-4180 — this is already covered by existing backend tests). However, the user action of clicking Apply (applying a partial state to the editor) or Discard (choosing not to apply) is not described as emitting any structured event to audit or telemetry. Apply is a frontend-only state mutation with no server round-trip (spec §7.6), so it would not appear in the audit trail unless a dedicated event is emitted. The plan should explicitly state whether recovery Apply/Discard interactions are intentionally non-audited, and if so, why the underlying compose-failure persistence is sufficient as the audit-of-record.
findings.evidence: Spec §7.6: 'Apply. Frontend overwrites in-memory editor state with partial_state. No server round-trip'. routes.py lines 4173-4180 confirm audit recording for the transcript fetch. Plan does not mention emitting telemetry or structlog events for Apply/Discard choices.
findings.recommendation: Add a comment to the Task 7 implementation step explicitly acknowledging: 'Apply is intentionally not a separate audit event — the composition_states row persisted during the failed compose turn IS the audit record. The user's Apply choice changes in-memory state only and will become durable if/when a subsequent successful compose or manual save fires.' If instead Apply should emit a telemetry event (not Landscape audit), add that to Task 3 Step 2.

findings.title: Manual smoke step 2 describes audit-grade access recording as an observation, but no test verifies the round-trip from the frontend trigger
findings.detail: Task 8 Step 2 says 'Transcript fetch hits include_tool_rows=true and records audit-grade access server-side'. This is a manually-verified observation during smoke testing, not a test assertion. The backend path is tested by test_endpoint_emits_audit_log_when_include_tool_rows_true in test_record_audit_grade_view.py. However, there is no integration test that exercises the full round-trip: RecoveryTranscript component renders → calls fetchRecoveryTranscript → GET /messages?include_tool_rows=true → audit_access_log row is written. This end-to-end path remains VER-only as identified in W2 of the prior review.
findings.evidence: Prior review W2: 'Recovery validation remains VER-only until a real failed compose flow is exercised end to end.' Task 7 Step 1 integration tests simulate the store-level sendMessage rejection but do not confirm the audit row is emitted downstream.
findings.recommendation: The Task 7 Step 1 integration test against the rendered app surface should include an assertion that, after the panel opens and RecoveryTranscript renders, the test server's audit_access_log contains a row for the session. This does not require a full PostgreSQL container; the existing TestClient + in-memory SQLite conftest pattern is sufficient.

findings.title: Empty diff case is present but null/undefined partial_state fields are not explicitly listed
findings.detail: Task 4 Step 1 covers 'empty state when current and partial match' and 'large-state sanity'. It does not explicitly list the case where partial_state has a null source (valid: pipeline without source), or nodes/edges as empty arrays (valid initial state). These are not bugs in the plan — they are subsumed by the general 'added/removed/changed' matrix — but an implementer could miss them without an explicit test case.
findings.evidence: Task 4 Step 1 test list does not mention null source field. CompositionState source field in the backend is typed as nullable.
findings.recommendation: Add 'null source in partial_state (valid pipeline state, not an error)' to the Task 4 Step 1 test list to make this case explicit for implementers.

findings.title: View raw transcript semantics for not-yet-redacted content are undefined
findings.detail: Spec §7.6 says 'View raw transcript. Expands to a read-only view of every redacted tool call/response JSON.' The plan implements this as 'Keep raw transcript expansion redacted-only; never render hidden chain-of-thought or provider payloads.' The term 'raw' in the button label could mislead users into expecting unredacted content. The plan does not clarify what happens if a message row has raw_content populated (from include_raw_content=true) — since fetchRecoveryTranscript does not pass include_raw_content, the field should always be absent, but the component should defensively assert this. This is an info-level concern: the plan is probably correct but the implementer should be aware.
findings.evidence: routes.py: include_raw_content is a separate query parameter from include_tool_rows. fetchRecoveryTranscript in Task 1 Step 3 does not include include_raw_content in the URL params.
findings.recommendation: Add a note to Task 5 Step 2 that fetchRecoveryTranscript must not pass include_raw_content and that RecoveryTranscript must not render raw_content even if the field is populated (e.g., if a future API change starts populating it by default).

findings.title: Feature flag / rollout strategy is not mentioned; always-on is implicit
findings.detail: The plan does not mention a feature flag or rollout gate for the recovery panel. This is defensible: the recovery panel is an error-only surface (it opens only when both partial_state and failed_turn are present in the error response, a path that is inert when no compose failure occurs). There is no behaviour change on the happy path. However, the plan should explicitly state this is an always-on feature with no flag, so a reviewer or operator does not assume a flag was accidentally omitted.
findings.evidence: Plan 'Phase 4 Done When' list does not mention a feature flag. Spec §7.2: 'The recovery panel opens iff response body carries partial_state AND failed_turn.' Overview done conditions also make no reference to flags.
findings.recommendation: Add one sentence to Task 7 Step 2 or the 'Phase 4 Done When' section: 'No feature flag is required. The recovery panel is gated entirely by the presence of partial_state and failed_turn in the compose error response and is inert on all non-error paths.'

findings.title: Prior review W2 — recovery validation remains VER-only — is not closed by this plan
findings.detail: The prior review identified W2: 'Recovery validation remains VER-only until a real failed compose flow is exercised end to end.' Phase 4 Task 8 Step 2 is a manual smoke test that exercises the flow, but it does not constitute an automated end-to-end test. The VAL owner is elspeth-90b4542b63, which the plan correctly cites. The plan's own 'Phase 4 Done When' item 10 explicitly defers this to that ticket. This is not a new finding — it is a forward reference to the same open concern.
findings.evidence: Plan 'Phase 4 Done When' item 10: 'VAL — the user can actually recover from a failure — remains owned by elspeth-90b4542b63.' Prior review W2 is unresolved.
findings.recommendation: No action required in this plan. Confirm elspeth-90b4542b63 is still open and carries the release:rc5 label before the Phase 4 PR is merged.

blocking_items: TS-1: Task 0 Step 5 verification scope is too narrow — runs only 6 files after a constructor refactor that affects 20+ test files
warning_items: TS-2: AST guard does not cover _PHASE3_ORIGINAL_COMPOSER_INIT module-level capture
TS-3: Task 1 Step 1 does not require asserting nested failed_turn sub-fields
TS-4: No test asserts fetchRecoveryTranscript URL contains include_tool_rows=true
TS-5: No test asserts redaction markers are rendered and raw sensitive content is not
TS-6: discardRecovery test does not require asserting recoveryError is cleared to null
TS-7: Focus restoration only tested for one close path; concurrent-edit cancel path omitted
TS-8: Task 9 final gate has no backend re-run — Phase 4A cleanup not verified at PR time
info_items: TS-9: Accessibility audit is manual only; no jest-axe or equivalent automated a11y gate
OBS-1: Apply and Discard actions emit no structured event; plan should explicitly state this is intentional
OBS-2: Audit-grade access round-trip from frontend to audit_access_log remains manually verified only
PR-1: Always-on behaviour (no feature flag) is implicit, not stated
PR-2: Prior review W2 recovery-VER-only remains open by design (VAL owner is elspeth-90b4542b63)
